chunks.text: The EECS Technical Memorandum Series provides a dated archive of EECS research. It includes Ph.D. theses and master's reports as well as technical documents that complement traditional publication media such as journals. For example, technical reports may document work in progress, early versions of results that are eventually published in more traditional media, and supplemental information such as long proofs, software documentation, code listings, or elaborated examples.
chunks.question: What does the EECS Technical Memorandum Series archive include?
chunks.answer: Ph.D. theses, master's reports, and technical documents.

chunks.text: Technical reports listed here include the EECS Technical Report series (started in October 2005), the CS Technical Report series (from 1982 to 2005), and the ERL Technical Report series (from 1962 to 2005). Links to the full text are included in almost all cases.
chunks.question: Which technical report series are listed on the page?

chunks.text: For more information, see the department policy for the technical reports. To submit a technical report, see the submission form.
